verdict: 2026-07-03 18:21:48 PDT - The sketch is mostly the right small shape: mock-gating the name field is sound, `myName` still flows from `seedPresenterName(self)` in real Zoom, and reusing `addRule` plus `clearOverride` avoids an unnecessary store action. I would not build the save helper exactly as sketched, though, because its duplicate check uses the resolved source as a proxy for saved-list membership, and that breaks on manual overrides over existing direct or alias matches.

findings.title: Save helper confuses current rate source with saved-list membership
findings.claim: `saveToListTarget(p)` returns a save payload for every participant whose current `source` is not `matched`. But `resolveParticipant` gives manual overrides precedence before direct or alias matching, so an attendee already in the private list can become `source: 'manual'` when overridden. The proposed UI would then show Save and call `addRule(displayName, rate)`, persisting a duplicate same-name rule or a conflicting alias-backed rule before `clearOverride` re-resolves it.
findings.alternative: Make saved-list membership an explicit predicate instead of deriving it from `source`: have the helper take `participant` plus `config` or precomputed indexes, reuse existing `normalizeName`, `buildRateIndex`, `buildAliasIndex`, and `SOURCE.MATCHED`, and return null for direct or alias-backed saved names regardless of current manual override. Keep the UI wiring to `actions.addRule(target.name, target.rate); actions.clearOverride(p.id);` only when that target is non-null.
findings.win: Prevents persistent duplicate/private-rate rows and alias conflicts while keeping the implementation pure, node-env testable, dependency-free, and within existing store semantics. Add helper tests for manual-over-direct-match, manual-over-alias-match, default unsaved, and manual unsaved.
